title: Guardrails and Troubleshooting
description: The most common rules and debugging paths when using Codex with the Anydocs MCP server.

render.markdown:
# Guardrails and Troubleshooting

Common rules to remember:

- always pass `projectRoot`
- `lang` must be enabled in the project
- `page_update` cannot change `status`
- publishing may be rejected if page approval rules are not satisfied

When something fails:

1. keep the original error, especially its `rule` and `remediation`
2. go back to `project_open` or `project_validate`
3. use `page_get` or `page_find` to confirm the target page really exists
4. do not bypass MCP by editing raw JSON just to get unstuck quickly

If those rules are written into `AGENTS.md`, Codex becomes much more predictable in daily authoring work.
render.plainText: Remember to always pass projectRoot and lang, keep status changes out of page_update, and expect publish rules to be enforced. When something fails, inspect the returned rule and remediation, validate the project again, and avoid bypassing MCP with raw JSON edits.
